title: Overblown one-word concepts - The German Language Column - Goethe ...
content: In her final column, Olga Grjasnowa zeroes in on two words that hold a special place in the German language: "Heimat" ("home" or "homeland"), an elusive concept that can be hard to get your head around, and "Wurzeln" ("roots"), which at least seems quite tangible. Both are heavily fraught with political and emotional connotations.
